mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or falling-rate periods show no statistically significant excess return versus flat/rising periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 20d forward returns, or the hit rate of negative returns following trigger events is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that compounds through the business cycle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. Neither series alone captures the compounding demand impulse; the interaction of both rising together is required.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce 60d forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series triggers alone explain equivalent predictive power (interaction term insignificant in regression).

mechanism_narrative: A rising 1-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which feeds through to near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-trading-day horizon as markets incrementally price in the data release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5d SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) below its trailing 12-month average, occurring specifically during a late-cycle expansion when FEDFUNDS is above its 2-year median, signals labor-market cooling that combines with tight monetary policy to increase recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect is regime-conditional: in early-cycle or easing regimes the same payroll deceleration is not predictive.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change falls below trailing 12-month average AND FEDFUNDS above its trailing 2-year median
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following joint trigger are not significantly more negative than returns following payroll deceleration alone during easing regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The real policy rate, approximated by FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation, exceeding 1.5% signals a genuinely restrictive monetary stance that compresses valuations and credit availability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. Below this threshold the restrictive channel is inactive regardless of nominal FEDFUNDS level.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during high real-rate trigger periods are not significantly lower than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 5-year subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month peak signals weakening residential investment demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. This mechanism is specific to periods when the housing sector is a meaningful share of GDP growth contribution, i.e., mid-to-late expansion regimes, and is muted during early-recovery regimes when starts are rebounding off a trough.
failure_condition: Median 60d forward return following trigger is not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the same magnitude decline during early-recovery regimes produces equally negative returns, invalidating regime specificity.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the trailing month reflects a synchronized supply-and-labor expansion that generates a short-lived risk-on impulse in equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon. Neither series alone reliably predicts the short-horizon move; the co-movement is the signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0.1%
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following joint trigger are not significantly higher than returns following either single-series trigger alone (ANOVA p > 0.05), indicating no genuine interaction effect.
